title: Extra Select Pond Pellets Complete Fish Food Tub, 5 Litre
features: Vitamin-Enriched: Boosts fish immune system, essential for koi food and goldfish food.
Promotes Vibrancy: Inclusion of spirulina algae in these fish pellets encourages a colourful pond.
Diverse Fish: Suitable as koi fish food or as general goldfish pellets with it's balanced nutrition.
Supports Growth: This pond fish food has 27% protein and packed with nutrients for healthy fish.
Feed Responsibility: Reduce feeding when your fish pond is cooler in autumn, and stop in winter.
description: From the manufacturer Extra Select: Pond Pellets Ingredients Cereals, derivatives of vegetable origin, meat and animal derivatives, fish and fish derivatives, minerals, oils and fats, algae. Analytical Crude Protein 27%, Crude Fats 4.5%, Crude Fibre 2.5%, Crude Ash 7.5% Feeding Guide Feed your outdoor fish 2-3 times daily in summer, once daily during spring and autumn, and halt feeding in the winter season. For indoor ponds, consistent feeding throughout the year is recommended. Always ensure that the fish consume all the pellets within a few minutes and remove any leftovers to prevent water pollution. Handy Tips Portion Control: Serve only as much as fish can eat shortly. Avoid Overfeeding: Excess food can harm water quality. Adapt Feeding: More in summer; reduce in spring/autumn; stop in winter. Year-Round Indoor Care: Suitable for all-season indoor feeding. More Digestible Extra Select Pond Pellets are designed for high digestibility, ensuring that your pond fish absorb maximum nutrition without the risk of digestive issues, making them ideal for maintaining a healthy pond environment. Balanced Nutrition These pellets are a powerhouse of essential nutrients, including Vitamins A, D, E, and C, along with a balanced mix of minerals. This comprehensive nutritional makeup supports overall fish health, growth, and vitality. Supports Healthy Fish Enhanced with a rich blend of vitamins and minerals, Extra Select Pond Pellets promote vigorous health and vitality in pond fish, ensuring they remain active and robust, especially in cooler water temperatures.
